categories.name: Conditions that increase vulnerability to heat stroke
What to do in case of a heatstroke

fun_fact: Heat is called "the silent killer" — it claims more lives globally than floods, storms, or earthquakes, yet rarely makes headlines. As India's summers get hotter and longer, heat stroke is a growing risk — especially for those working outdoors or without access to cooling. [Learn what exactly happens to the body in extreme heat →](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uuWupBQOms8)
